summary: Filter Conversation Agent transcript noise out of model input.
rationale: This follow-up keeps runtime progress and command-state transcript items visible to the Workbench UI while preventing those operational records from becoming future LLM context. The change belongs in the Conversation Agent store/service contract because the business transcript is the canonical source of truth, not an OpenAI Agents SDK session transcript.
line_count_rationale: The service file is already above the production line-count redline. This PR changes the existing runtime-progress append path and adds focused regression coverage instead of introducing a parallel transcript adapter during release hardening.
deletion_targets: runtime progress messages reused as LLM recent transcript context
command-state messages reused as LLM recent transcript context
runtime progress duplicate handling by broad sqlite UNIQUE exception swallowing
risks: Runtime progress must remain visible in UI transcript responses even when excluded from model input.
Idempotency must deduplicate repeated runtime event polling without hiding unrelated database integrity failures.
Future message types need explicit model-input inclusion semantics instead of accidental defaults.
